Lines.FullText: Represents the client-side part of a streaming service responsible for initiating

Elements.RawText: Represents the client-side part of a streaming service responsible for initiating

Lines.FullText: communication with the openDAQ device streaming server and processing the received data.

Elements.RawText: communication with the openDAQ device streaming server and processing the received data.

Lines.FullText: Wraps the client-side implementation details of the particular data transfer protocol used by

Elements.RawText: Wraps the client-side implementation details of the particular data transfer protocol used by

Lines.FullText: openDAQ to send processed/acquired data from devices running an openDAQ Server to an

Elements.RawText: openDAQ to send processed/acquired data from devices running an openDAQ Server to an

Lines.FullText: openDAQ Client.

Elements.RawText: openDAQ Client.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief Represents the client-side part of a streaming service responsible for initiating
 * communication with the openDAQ device streaming server and processing the received data.
 * Wraps the client-side implementation details of the particular data transfer protocol used by
 * openDAQ to send processed/acquired data from devices running an openDAQ Server to an
 * openDAQ Client.

Lines.FullText: Usually, the data transfer protocol provides information about the signals whose data can be sent

Elements.RawText: Usually, the data transfer protocol provides information about the signals whose data can be sent

Lines.FullText: over the protocol. It allows the implementation to reject unsupported signals from being added to

Elements.RawText: over the protocol. It allows the implementation to reject unsupported signals from being added to

Lines.FullText: the streaming. Each Streaming object provides the string representation of a connection address

Elements.RawText: the streaming. Each Streaming object provides the string representation of a connection address

Lines.FullText: used to connect to the streaming service of the device. This string representation is used as

Elements.RawText: used to connect to the streaming service of the device. This string representation is used as

Lines.FullText: a unique ID to determine the streaming source for the mirrored signal.

Elements.RawText: a unique ID to determine the streaming source for the mirrored signal.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Usually, the data transfer protocol provides information about the signals whose data can be sent
 * over the protocol. It allows the implementation to reject unsupported signals from being added to
 * the streaming. Each Streaming object provides the string representation of a connection address
 * used to connect to the streaming service of the device. This string representation is used as
 * a unique ID to determine the streaming source for the mirrored signal.

Lines.FullText: When the generalized client-to-device streaming mechanism is employed, however, the roles are effectively switched:

Elements.RawText: When the generalized client-to-device streaming mechanism is employed, however, the roles are effectively switched:

Lines.FullText: the server becomes a consumer of signal data, while the client-side Streaming object acts as

Elements.RawText: the server becomes a consumer of signal data, while the client-side Streaming object acts as

Lines.FullText: the producer, sending signal data over the protocol. In this context, Streaming objects are expected

Elements.RawText: the producer, sending signal data over the protocol. In this context, Streaming objects are expected

Lines.FullText: to exist on the server side as well to interact with mirrored copies of client signals.

Elements.RawText: to exist on the server side as well to interact with mirrored copies of client signals.

Lines.FullText: In context of client-to-device streaming, in addition to signals, mirrored input ports can also be added or removed

Elements.RawText: In context of client-to-device streaming, in addition to signals, mirrored input ports can also be added or removed

Lines.FullText: dynamically through the corresponding methods of the Streaming interface, similarly to mirrored signals.

Elements.RawText: dynamically through the corresponding methods of the Streaming interface, similarly to mirrored signals.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * When the generalized client-to-device streaming mechanism is employed, however, the roles are effectively switched:
 * the server becomes a consumer of signal data, while the client-side Streaming object acts as
 * the producer, sending signal data over the protocol. In this context, Streaming objects are expected
 * to exist on the server side as well to interact with mirrored copies of client signals.
 * In context of client-to-device streaming, in addition to signals, mirrored input ports can also be added or removed
 * dynamically through the corresponding methods of the Streaming interface, similarly to mirrored signals.

Lines.FullText: The support for client-to-device streaming can be checked via `getClientToDeviceStreamingEnabled`.

Elements.RawText: The support for client-to-device streaming can be checked via `getClientToDeviceStreamingEnabled`.

Lines.FullText: If it is not enabled or not supported by the protocol, the method provides `false` value, and adding or removing

Elements.RawText: If it is not enabled or not supported by the protocol, the method provides `false` value, and adding or removing

Lines.FullText: mirrored input ports is not allowed, therefore disabling any client-to-device streaming operations.

Elements.RawText: mirrored input ports is not allowed, therefore disabling any client-to-device streaming operations.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The support for client-to-device streaming can be checked via `getClientToDeviceStreamingEnabled`.
 * If it is not enabled or not supported by the protocol, the method provides `false` value, and adding or removing
 * mirrored input ports is not allowed, therefore disabling any client-to-device streaming operations.

Lines.FullText: The Streaming is used as a selectable data source for mirrored signals. For this, it

Elements.RawText: The Streaming is used as a selectable data source for mirrored signals. For this, it

Lines.FullText: provides methods, allowing mirrored signals to be added/removed dynamically,

Elements.RawText: provides methods, allowing mirrored signals to be added/removed dynamically,

Lines.FullText: to enable/disable the use of Streaming as a data source of these signals.

Elements.RawText: to enable/disable the use of Streaming as a data source of these signals.

Lines.FullText: Forwarding of packets received from the remote device through the data transfer protocol down to

Elements.RawText: Forwarding of packets received from the remote device through the data transfer protocol down to

Lines.FullText: the signal path is enabled when the following conditions are met:

Elements.RawText: the signal path is enabled when the following conditions are met:

Lines.FullText: - Streaming object itself is in active state.

Elements.RawText: - Streaming object itself is in active state.

Lines.FullText: - Streaming is selected as an active source of the corresponding signal.

Elements.RawText: - Streaming is selected as an active source of the corresponding signal.

Description.RawText:
*
 * The Streaming is used as a selectable data source for mirrored signals. For this, it
 * provides methods, allowing mirrored signals to be added/removed dynamically,
 * to enable/disable the use of Streaming as a data source of these signals.
 * Forwarding of packets received from the remote device through the data transfer protocol down to
 * the signal path is enabled when the following conditions are met:
 * - Streaming object itself is in active state.
 * - Streaming is selected as an active source of the corresponding signal.
